The bar itself is subtle: about 32–40px tall with 12–16px of vertical padding and a light bottom border or subtle rule to separate it from the first paragraph, with the “Last updated” label left-aligned within it. There is 16–24px of space between the lower edge of this bar and the start of the terms content, giving the metadata breathing room without overshadowing the body text.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main content flow, snapping under the hero and above the terms content. It scales with the main content column width and reflows to remain full-width within that column on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is an invisible behavioral layer associated with the main terms content column. Spatially, it is attached to the vertical span of the terms-content-renderer, conceptually tracking the positions of section headings from the top of the main content area down to its end. While it does not render any visible UI itself, it maps the scroll position within the right-hand text column and could be used to drive subtle state changes such as URL hashes or highlighting in a separate navigation context, all without introducing any additional visual elements or altering the column’s layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendered and non-positioned; it observes scrolling within the main content area and maintains internal state only. It scrolls along with the document purely as logic bound to the vertical extent of the terms content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The header navigation forms a slim, fixed bar anchored to the very top of the viewport, spanning the full browser width. It is approximately 56–64px tall, with its content horizontally centered within the same max-width container used by the main content. Inside this bar, the “Jack Hacks” logo sits at the far left, slightly inset (about 24px from the left container edge), while the main navigation links are aligned to the right, spaced evenly with 16–24px between them. The bar overlays the top of the hero but has its own solid dark background color to ensure contrast and readability over the moody hero image. Below it, the hero begins immediately, with the title visually separated by 64–80px of vertical space. On scroll, the header remains pinned at the top edge, providing a consistent anchor point while the hero and main content glide beneath it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed to the top of the viewport with full-width horizontal span. Its internal content is constrained to the central max-width container, and it stays visible at all times as the rest of the page scrolls underneath.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The footer navigation sits at the very bottom of the page, stretching full-width across the viewport and echoing the clean, minimal feel of the header. It is separated from the end of the terms content renderer by a generous 48–64px of vertical whitespace to clearly mark the end of the legal document. Within the footer, content is centered in the same max-width container as the main body. Links (such as secondary navigation or legal references) are typically centered or right-aligned depending on the site’s global pattern, with subtle typography and minimal decoration. The footer height is around 48–64px, with 16–24px of vertical padding. A light top border or color shift differentiates it from the main content area without introducing heavy visual weight, maintaining the flat, modern aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the bottom of the document, appearing after all scrolling content. It does not stick or float; it becomes visible only once the user scrolls past the main terms content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The inline link handler is non-visual and is conceptually overlaid on the text inside the terms-content-renderer. It attaches to all inline links within the legal text (such as references to other policies or external resources) but does not alter their visual placement in the column. All links remain in their natural positions within the left-aligned text flow, with standard styling (e.g., subtle color change and perhaps an underline) to match the site’s minimal aesthetic. The handler’s presence is purely behavioral: it ensures that when a link is clicked, the interaction is handled consistently, without adding any visible UI elements around the links.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Invisible and non-positioned; it occupies no space and simply binds to inline anchor elements wherever they appear within the scrolling terms content column.
